title: Feature Implementation Guide — NxGN Actions API
purpose: **AI Summary**: End-to-end guide for implementing a management API feature in NxGN Actions. Covers the 11-step implementation order, canonical save command flow, runtime data-flow narratives using WorkflowDefinition as the primary example, a decision tree for feature complexity, a complete checklist, and approved deviations from Capstone.

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 1
examples.code:
HTTP POST /api/v1/admin/workflows
  │ body: { id: "00000000-...", name: "Corrective Action", code: "CAR", states: [...] }
  ▼
SaveWorkflowRequestValidator            ← Shape validation (lengths, formats, enum ranges)
  │ passes
  ▼
SaveWorkflowEndpoint                    ← Thin orchestrator (TenantEndpoint base extracts
  │                                       TenantId, UserId, RequestLanguageId, DefaultLanguageId)
  │ calls
  ▼
SaveWorkflowCommand                     ← Business logic orchestration
  │ calls
  ▼
WorkflowEntityMapper.MapAsync(          ← Batch-primary: List<SaveWorkflowRequest>, userId,
    [request], userId,                    requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId, ct, db
    requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId,
    ct, db)
  │ Determines create vs update:
  │   dto.Id == Guid.Empty → create (Guid.CreateVersion7())
  │   dto.Id != Guid.Empty → find by ID → update
  │ Maps scalar fields, enums (EnumDTO.Id → C# enum cast), audit fields
  │ Handles translations via TranslationService.AddOrUpdateEntityTranslation
  │ returns EntityMapperResult<WorkflowDefinition> { NewItems | UpdatedItems }
  ▼
SaveCommand continues:
  │ 1. Check empty result (not found on update) → return Fail(404)
  │ 2. AddRange(mapResult.NewItems) if new
  │ 2. Protection checks (immutable Code field on update)
  │ 3. Uniqueness check (Code unique per tenant — eager check, DB index is final guard)
  │ 4. Child diffing (load existing states, compute add/update/delete sets)
  │ 5. Business validation (state machine integrity, at least one initial state)
  │ 6. Process children (apply add/update/delete for states and transitions)
  │ 7. SaveChangesAsync + TOCTOU catch on unique constraint
  │ 8. Post-save side effects (cache invalidation — each in try/catch)
  │ returns CommandResult<EntityMapperResult<WorkflowDefinition>>
  ▼
SaveEndpoint continues:
  │ 1. Check result.Succeeded → if not, SendProblemAsync + return
  │ 2. Check result.Value!.NewItems.Count > 0 → 201 Created, else → 200 OK
  │ 2. Audit log (workflow.created or workflow.updated)
  │ 3. Return SaveWorkflowResponse(Id)
  ▼
HTTP 201 / 200 { "id": "..." }

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 2
examples.code:
HTTP GET /api/v1/admin/workflows/{id}
  │
  ▼
GetWorkflowEndpoint                     ← TenantEndpointWithoutRequest<WorkflowDefinitionDTO>
  │ calls
  ▼
GetWorkflowQuery                        ← AsNoTracking, AsSplitQuery, Includes, TagWith
  │ returns WorkflowDefinition? (with States, Transitions loaded)
  ▼
WorkflowDTOMapper.Map(                  ← Entity → DTO
    entity,                                TranslationService resolves Name/Description
    RequestLanguageId,                     per requestLanguageId with defaultLanguageId fallback
    DefaultLanguageId)                     EnumDTO for enums, NamedDTO for FK refs
  │ returns WorkflowDefinitionDTO
  ▼
HTTP 200 { ...dto } or HTTP 404

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 3
examples.code:
HTTP POST /api/v1/admin/workflows/grid-list  ← POST because filter payload is complex
  │ body: { page, pageSize, search, status }
  ▼
GetWorkflowGridListEndpoint             ← TenantEndpoint<PagedRequest, GridListResponse<T>>
  │ builds WorkflowQueryParameters from PagedRequest
  │ fires two queries in parallel (Task.WhenAll)
  ├──▶ GetWorkflowsQuery               ← Paged data (QueryParameters → ApplyFilters → OrderBy → ApplyPagination)
  └──▶ GetWorkflowsCountQuery          ← Total count (same QueryParameters, no pagination)
  │ both return
  ▼
DTOMapper.MapGridItem(entities, requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId)
  │ batch mapping — flat grid-optimized DTO per row
  ▼
HTTP 200 { items: [...], totalCount, page, pageSize }

examples.name: How Data Flows at Runtime Example 4
examples.code:
HTTP POST /api/v1/admin/workflows/delete
  │ body: { entityKeys: [guid1, guid2, ...] }
  ▼
DeleteWorkflowEndpoint
  │ calls
  ▼
DeleteWorkflowCommand
  │ 1. Protection check (system-protected workflows) → return CommandResult.Fail(...)
  │ 2. Begin explicit transaction
  │ 3. ExecuteDeleteAsync on child tables (transitions → states → root)
  │ 4. ExecuteDeleteAsync on root table
  │ 5. Commit transaction
  │ returns CommandResult.Success
  ▼
Endpoint:
  │ if (!result.Succeeded) → SendProblemAsync(result.Error) → return
  │ Audit each key → 204 No Content
  ▼
HTTP 204 No Content

examples.name: Not every feature requires every piece. Use this decision tree
examples.code:
Is this a new entity?
├── YES → Entity + EF Configuration (IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>) + Migration
└── NO  → Skip to Contracts

Does the entity need CRUD?
├── YES → Full set: DTO, SaveRequest, Response, GridItemDTO, DeleteRequest
│         EntityMapper, DTOMapper, all queries, SaveCommand, DeleteCommand
│         Save/Get/GridList/Delete endpoints + validators
└── NO  → Only the specific operations needed

Does the entity have children (aggregate root)?
├── YES → Child diffing in SaveCommand (NOT in mapper)
│         Child DTOs in parent DTO
│         Child collections with Guid.Empty detection for new children
│         EntityMap<T, TTranslation> lookups in mapper for child resolution
└── NO  → Simple single-entity save

Does the entity implement ITranslatedEntity<T>?
├── YES → TranslationService in EntityMapper (AddOrUpdateEntityTranslation)
│         TranslationService in DTOMapper (GetEntityTranslation, BuildTranslatedValues)
│         requestLanguageId + defaultLanguageId on all mapper method signatures
│         TranslatedValueDTO list on the DTO for translation editing UI
└── NO  → No translation parameters needed

Does the entity implement ISoftDeletable?
├── YES → Delete command sets IsDeleted/DeletedDate/DeletedById instead of ExecuteDeleteAsync
│         EF global query filter excludes soft-deleted records
│         Consider "restore" lifecycle endpoint
└── NO  → Physical delete with ExecuteDeleteAsync

Does the entity implement IVersioned?
├── YES → Version field as EF concurrency token
│         Optimistic concurrency check on save
│         Version returned in DTO for client-side conflict detection
└── NO  → No concurrency control

Does the entity need lifecycle commands (activate, disable, approve)?
├── YES → Lifecycle command (domain-specific result)
│         Lifecycle endpoint + validator
└── NO  → Skip

Does the DTOMapper need data beyond the entity itself?
├── 0 extra types → Simple: mapper.Map(entity, requestLangId, defaultLangId)
├── 1 extra type  → Direct parameter: mapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang, extraData)
└── 2+ extra types → DTOMapperDataContext + DTOMapperDataContextService
                      (parallel loading via Task.WhenAll)

Does the entity need Excel export?
├── YES → Export endpoint, ExcelService integration
│         Grid item DTO reused for export rows
└── NO  → Skip

examples.name: Implementation Order Example 1
examples.code:
1. Entity (if new) — TrackedEntity, ITenantEntity, ITranslatedEntity<T>, ISoftDeletable, IVersioned
 2. EF Configuration — IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>, "actions" schema, ConfigureTrackedEntity/ConfigureTenantEntity
 3. Migration — dotnet-ef migrations add {Name}
 4. Contracts — DTO (inherits KeyedDTO or NamedDTO), SaveRequest (inherits DTO), Response, GridItemDTO, DeleteRequest
 5. EntityMapper — DI-registered, MapAsync with List<DTO> batch primary, userId + requestLanguageId + defaultLanguageId
 6. DTOMapper — DI-registered, TranslationService in constructor, language IDs on methods
 7. QueryParameters — extends PagedQueryParameters<T> with entity-specific filters
 8. Queries — Get, Grid, GridCount (use QueryParameters), Lookup if needed
 9. SaveCommand — uses EntityMapper, AddRange/UpdateRange, returns CommandResult<EntityMapperResult<T>>
10. DeleteCommand — returns CommandResult, protection checks return CommandResult.Fail(...)
11. Endpoints + Validators — TenantEndpoint base, EntityMapperResult handling, audit logging
12. DI Registration — Add{Feature}Services() in Bootstrap/Features/

Does the entity have translated Name/Description (via ITranslatedEntity<T>)?
├── YES → inherit NamedDTO  (e.g., ActionDTO : NamedDTO, WorkflowDefinitionDTO : NamedDTO)
│         Name/Description filled by DTOMapper via TranslationService
│
Does the entity have Id (Guid), Name (string), Description (string?) as direct properties?
├── YES → inherit NamedDTO  (e.g., LanguageDTO : NamedDTO)
└── NO  → inherit KeyedDTO
           ├── Name field exists but called something else? → KeyedDTO
           └── Has extra base fields beyond Name/Description? → KeyedDTO

Features/Admin/Workflows/
├── Save/
│   ├── SaveWorkflowEndpoint.cs

├── Get/
│   └── GetWorkflowEndpoint.cs
├── GetGridList/

│   └── GetWorkflowLookupListEndpoint.cs

    │   └── WorkflowQueryParameters.cs         # Extends PagedQueryParameters<T>
    ├── Commands/
    │   ├── SaveWorkflowCommand.cs             # Returns CommandResult<EntityMapperResult<T>>
    │   ├── DeleteWorkflowCommand.cs           # Returns CommandResult
    │   └── ActivateWorkflowCommand.cs         # Returns CommandResult or CommandResult<T>
    └── Mappers/
        ├── WorkflowEntityMapper.cs            # DI-registered, MapAsync → EntityMapperResult<T>
        ├── WorkflowDTOMapper.cs               # DI-registered, Map/MapGridItem with TranslationService
        └── WorkflowDTOMapperDataContext.cs     # (if 2+ extra data types needed)

Nxgn.Actions.Contracts/Workflows/
├── WorkflowDefinitionDTO.cs                   # { get; set; }, inherits NamedDTO
├── WorkflowDefinitionGridItemDTO.cs           # { get; set; }, flat subset
├── WorkflowStateDTO.cs                        # { get; set; }, child DTO
├── SaveWorkflowRequest.cs                     # inherits WorkflowDefinitionDTO, Guid.Empty
├── SaveWorkflowStateRequest.cs                # child save request
├── SaveWorkflowResponse.cs                    # sealed record
└── DeleteWorkflowsRequest.cs                  # sealed record, Guid[] EntityKeys

Bootstrap/Features/
└── WorkflowsExtensions.cs                     # Add{Feature}Services() extension method

rules.rule_text: Admin aggregate modules must implement GET, SAVE, DELETE, grid-list, and lookups endpoints unless the design explicitly states why one is not applicable.

Each endpoint slice must have contract DTOs in Nxgn.Actions.Contracts, request validation where applicable, command/query handling, pure mapper usage, permission enforcement, mutation audit, and endpoint-oriented tests.
DbContext-backed commands and queries must use IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext> and accept optional ActionsDbContext? dbContext for caller-owned transaction participation.
Do not define browser-only public DTO shapes inside the API project; public API contracts belong in Nxgn.Actions.Contracts.
Lookup endpoints that feed another permission-gated capture flow must have authorization tests for each consuming module permission, not only the lookup owner's maintenance permission.
Do not omit a lookup endpoint when another module or capture page needs foreign-key options from the owning aggregate.

sections.title: How Data Flows at Runtime
content: Before building anything, understand how a request moves through the system. Every management API operation follows one of these flows. WorkflowDefinition is used as the primary example because it has children (states, transitions), translations (`ITranslatedEntity<T>`), and is tenant-scoped.
│ body: { id: "00000000-...", name: "Corrective Action", code: "CAR", states: [...] }
SaveWorkflowRequestValidator            ← Shape validation (lengths, formats, enum ranges)
│   dto.Id != Guid.Empty → find by ID → update
│ Maps scalar fields, enums (EnumDTO.Id → C# enum cast), audit fields
│ 1. Check empty result (not found on update) → return Fail(404)
│ 2. Protection checks (immutable Code field on update)
│ 3. Uniqueness check (Code unique per tenant — eager check, DB index is final guard)
│ 4. Child diffing (load existing states, compute add/update/delete sets)
│ 5. Business validation (state machine integrity, at least one initial state)
│ 6. Process children (apply add/update/delete for states and transitions)
│ 7. SaveChangesAsync + TOCTOU catch on unique constraint
│ 8. Post-save side effects (cache invalidation — each in try/catch)
│ 1. Check result.Succeeded → if not, SendProblemAsync + return
RequestLanguageId,                     per requestLanguageId with defaultLanguageId fallback
DefaultLanguageId)                     EnumDTO for enums, NamedDTO for FK refs
HTTP POST /api/v1/admin/workflows/grid-list  ← POST because filter payload is complex
├──▶ GetWorkflowsQuery               ← Paged data (QueryParameters → ApplyFilters → OrderBy → ApplyPagination)
└──▶ GetWorkflowsCountQuery          ← Total count (same QueryParameters, no pagination)
│ batch mapping — flat grid-optimized DTO per row
│ 3. ExecuteDeleteAsync on child tables (transitions → states → root)

sections.title: Admin Configuration Module Minimum Surface
content: A user-configurable admin module is not complete with only persistence. It must expose the standard management API surface: GET for aggregate load, SAVE for aggregate upsert, DELETE for reference-safe removal, POST grid-list for server-side grids, and GET lookups for foreign-key consumers. Endpoint slices use contract DTOs, validators, commands or queries, pure mappers, permission enforcement, audit for mutations, and tests. DbContext-backed commands and queries follow ADR-CORE-0027 by using IDbContextFactory and accepting an optional shared ActionsDbContext.

sections.title: What Does My Feature Need?
content: Not every feature requires every piece. Use this decision tree:
Is this a new feature-owned aggregate or entity?
│         Register configuration through ActionsDbContext exactly once
└── NO  -> Skip to contracts and endpoints
Does the entity expose a public API shape?
│         Prefer module-level files such as Admin/WorkflowDTOs.cs while readable
└── NO  -> Keep internal request/command types inside the API feature
Does the entity need a server-side grid?
│         Optional /grid or /server-grid aliases only for compatibility
└── NO  -> Use lookup/query endpoints only when needed
Does the save request update an aggregate with children?
├── YES -> One aggregate save command owns child diffing and validation
│         Child DTOs ride inside the parent request
│         Guid.Empty means create, non-empty Guid means update
Is the request-specific command small and used by one endpoint?
├── YES -> Keep it in the endpoint folder/file
└── NO  -> Extract to Shared/Commands, Shared/Queries, Shared/Mappers, or a feature-local service
Does the entity implement ITranslatedEntity<T>, ISoftDeletable, IVersioned, or tenant scoping?
├── YES -> Apply the established entity/configuration/test conventions for that concern
└── NO  -> Do not add unused infrastructure
The rule is feature ownership first, extraction second. Add abstractions when they improve reuse, complexity, or testability.

sections.title: Implementation Order
content: 2. Feature-owned entities/configurations under {Feature}/Shared/ if new persistence is needed
6. Endpoint-specific command and validator for save/delete/lifecycle behavior
9. Unit/integration tests for validation, save semantics, grid-list behavior, and authorization
10. Browser plan or browser E2E coverage for the user workflow

Implement in this order so the public contract, persistence shape, and endpoint behavior are visible before the Angular client depends on them. Extract command/query/mapper files only when the feature code becomes reused or hard to test in place.

sections.title: Layer 1: Entity
content: See [Entity Pattern](api-entity.md) for full conventions. Key points:
- Feature-owned entities live under the owning feature, usually `{Feature}/Shared/*Entities.cs`.
- Inherit `TrackedEntity` for aggregate roots, `Entity` for lightweight join/value entities.
- Implement `ISoftDeletable` only when physical deletion is not acceptable.
public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public WorkflowDefinition WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;

sections.title: Layer 2: EF Configuration
content: See [EF Configuration Pattern](api-ef-configuration.md) for full conventions. One `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` per entity is still the default; feature-owned configurations may live under the feature's `Shared/` folder.
- Register each feature-owned configuration exactly once through the DbContext model configuration path.
- Use explicit relationships and indexes; do not rely on EF conventions for aggregate boundaries.
- Store enums as strings with `HasConversion<string>()` unless an existing schema requires otherwise.

sections.title: Layer 3: Contracts
content: All public DTOs, requests, and responses live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`. The current convention is module-level files, not one folder per entity:
Use per-type files only when the module file becomes too large to review safely.
public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public new List<SaveWorkflowStateRequest> States { get; set; } = [];
| DTO mutability | Classes with `{ get; set; }` properties |
| Save shape | Save request may inherit the read DTO when the shape matches |
| Enum fields | Use `EnumDTO` when serialized to the client |
| FK display references | Use `NamedDTO` when the UI displays a named reference |

sections.title: Layer 4: EntityMapper (DI-Registered)
content: The EntityMapper is a **DI-registered scoped service** that takes `List<DTO>` (batch primary) with `userId`, `requestLanguageId`, `defaultLanguageId` params. Returns `EntityMapperResult<T>`. See [EntityMapper Pattern](entity-mapper.md) for full conventions.
**What the EntityMapper does and does NOT do:**
- Maps request fields to entity fields (including EnumDTO.Id → C# enum cast)
- Determines create vs update via `dto.Id == Guid.Empty`
- Returns `EntityMapperResult<T>` with populated NewItems or UpdatedItems
- Does **NOT** diff children — that happens in the command
- Does **NOT** call SaveChangesAsync — the command owns persistence
- Does **NOT** perform business validation — the command owns rules
- Does **NOT** call AddRange — the command applies the result to DbContext
// Code is immutable after creation — not mapped on update
=> MapAsync([dto], userId, requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId, ct, db);
// CORRECT — Batch-primary signature with language params, returns EntityMapperResult<T>
// WRONG — Single-entity only, no language params (Identity pattern)
var mapResult = await entityMapper.MapAsync(request, userId, db, ct);

sections.title: Layer 5: DTOMapper (DI-Registered)
content: The DTOMapper is a **DI-registered scoped service** with `TranslationService` injected via primary constructor. Language IDs are passed as method parameters. See [DTOMapper Pattern](dto-mapper.md) for full conventions.
**What the DTOMapper does and does NOT do:**
- Transforms loaded entities into DTOs (pure function of its inputs)
- Resolves translations via `TranslationService.GetEntityTranslation` with fallback chain
- Constructs `EnumDTO` from C# enums, `NamedDTO` from FK navigations
- Builds `TranslatedValueDTO` lists for translation editing UI
- Maps child collections via child mapping methods
- Does **NOT** query the database — all data must be passed in
- Does **NOT** filter or sort — the query does that
// CORRECT — TranslationService via constructor, language IDs as method params
// WRONG — No TranslationService, direct entity.Name access
return new WorkflowDefinitionDTO { Name = entity.Name }; // skips translations
// WRONG — LINQ Select with static extension in endpoint

sections.title: Layer 6: QueryParameters
content: Extends `PagedQueryParameters<T>` with entity-specific filters. The base class provides `WithKeys()`, `WithPagination()`, `WithTracking()`, `ApplyFilters()`, and `ApplyPagination()`. RLS handles tenant isolation — no TenantId in the query parameters.
public string? Search { get; private set; }
public bool? IsActive { get; private set; }
// CORRECT — Extends PagedQueryParameters<T>, fluent WithXxx methods, override ApplyFilters
// WRONG — Standalone class without PagedQueryParameters base, manual TenantId filtering
public class WorkflowQueryParameters(PagedRequest request, Guid tenantId)
query = query.Where(w => w.TenantId == tenantId);  // WRONG: RLS handles this

sections.title: Layer 7: Queries
content: All queries use `IDbContextFactory<ActionsDbContext>`, `AsNoTracking` (default via PagedQueryParameters), `AsSplitQuery`, and `TagWith`. See [Query Pattern](queries.md) for full conventions.
Guid id, CancellationToken ct, ActionsDbContext? db = null)

sections.title: Layer 8: SaveCommand (Upsert)
content: The SaveCommand orchestrates: EntityMapper for mapping, AddRange for new entities, protection checks, uniqueness, child diffing, persistence, and post-save side effects. Returns `EntityMapperResult<T>`.
Every SaveCommand follows this sequence. The numbered steps are the canonical order — skip any that don't apply:
| 2. AddRange | `db.AddRange(mapResult.NewItems)` for new entities | No new items |
| 3. Protection | Immutability guards, core entity protection | No immutable fields or protected entities |
| 4. Uniqueness | Eager check for human-readable error, DB index is final guard | No unique constraints |
| 5. Child diffing | Load existing children, compute add/update/delete sets | No children (simple entity) |
| 6. Business validation | Domain rules (state machine integrity, FK existence, max limits) | No business rules beyond shape |
| 7. Process children | Apply child add/update/delete to DbContext | No children |
| 8. SaveChangesAsync + TOCTOU | `SaveChangesAsync` with catch for unique constraint races | Never — always needed |
| 9. Post-save effects | Cache invalidation, external cleanup | No side effects |
// Step 1: EntityMapper handles create/update detection and field mapping
// Step 2: Check for empty result (entity not found on update)
// Step 4: Protection checks (immutable Code on update)
"WORKFLOW-003", "Workflow code cannot be changed after creation.", 422);
// Step 5: Uniqueness check (eager, DB index is final guard)
// Step 5: Child diffing (inline, NOT in mapper)
"WORKFLOW-004", "Workflow must have at least one initial state.", 422);
// Step 10: Post-save side effects (best-effort, each in try/catch)
logger.LogWarning(ex, "Failed post-save side effect for workflow {WorkflowId}",
// CORRECT — CommandResult<EntityMapperResult<T>>, empty check, AddRange, return Ok
// WRONG — Raw EntityMapperResult without CommandResult wrapping (no error path)
public async Task<OneOf<Created<Guid>, Updated<Guid>, NotFound>> ExecuteAsync(...)
// WRONG — No language params passed to mapper
var mapResult = await entityMapper.MapAsync(request, userId, db, ct);
// The mapper is a pure DTO-to-entity transformer — no aggregate logic

sections.title: Layer 9: DeleteCommand
content: Delete commands accept `Guid[] keys` and return `CommandResult`. Protection checks return `CommandResult.Fail(...)`. Uses `ExecuteDeleteAsync` with explicit transactions.
// Explicit transaction — prevents partial deletes on failure
await using var tx = await db.Database.BeginTransactionAsync(ct);
// CORRECT — ExecuteDeleteAsync, explicit transaction, CommandResult return
await using var tx = await db.Database.BeginTransactionAsync(ct);
// WRONG — Void return with thrown exceptions for expected outcomes
throw new ValidationException("Cannot delete protected workflows...");

sections.title: Layer 10: Endpoints
content: Endpoints are thin orchestrators. Call command/query, handle result, audit, respond. All management endpoints extend `TenantEndpoint` base classes that extract `TenantId`, `UserId`, `RequestLanguageId`, `DefaultLanguageId` from JWT claims. Save endpoints use `EntityMapperResult<T>` for result handling. Delete/lifecycle endpoints check `CommandResult.Succeeded` and call `SendProblemAsync` on failure.
if (workflow is null) { await Send.NotFoundAsync(ct); return; }
| **Audit** | After successful save/delete, inside result handling branch |
| **DTO mapping** | DI-registered DTOMapper with language params: `dtoMapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang)` |
| **Primary constructors** | On all endpoints, commands, queries, mappers |

sections.title: Layer 11: Validators
content: Shape validation only. Business rules belong in commands.
| Field lengths, required, format | Uniqueness, FK existence |
| Enum range, child shape | Immutability guards, state machine integrity |
| No DB access | TOCTOU race handling |

sections.title: DI Registration
content: Each feature registers its services via a static extension method in `Bootstrap/Features/`. A central `Services.cs` chains all feature registrations. All services use `AddScoped`.
public static IServiceCollection AddWorkflowsServices(this IServiceCollection services)

sections.title: Feature File Structure
content: This is the implemented shape for the newer aggregate modules. Older examples that place every entity in `Data/Entities` or every command in `Shared/Commands` should be treated as historical unless the artifact is truly shared or reused.

sections.title: Complete Feature Checklist
content: - [ ] Each configuration is registered exactly once through `ActionsDbContext`
- [ ] Migration created and applied when schema changes
- [ ] Tenant, translation, soft-delete, and versioning interfaces are added only when needed
- [ ] Public DTOs and requests live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`
- [ ] Module-level DTO files remain readable; split per type only when needed
- [ ] Save request clearly documents create/update semantics (`Guid.Empty` creates)
- [ ] Grid response uses `PagedResponse<T>`/grid contract expected by the frontend
- [ ] Endpoint folders own request-specific handler, validator, and command/orchestration code
- [ ] Reusable feature logic is extracted under feature `Shared/`
- [ ] `/grid` or `/server-grid` aliases are registered only for compatibility
- [ ] No new `/list` grid routes are introduced
- [ ] Multi-write saves use the EF execution strategy when opening explicit transactions
- [ ] Authorization, tenant context, validation, and audit logging are covered
- [ ] List page uses the shared grid component and server-side data source
- [ ] Single-route aggregate editor keeps aggregate state in the routed component
- [ ] Multi-route aggregate editor uses capture state as the client-side aggregate root
- [ ] Feature service names match backend route semantics (`getGridList`, `save`, `getById`, lookup methods)
- [ ] Validation mirrors server rules without replacing server validation
- [ ] Integration tests cover create/update, aggregate child diffing, validation failures, and grid-list behavior
- [ ] Unit tests cover contract compatibility and pure client-side state where relevant
- [ ] Browser E2E sets up representative client data through the frontend when the workflow is user-facing
- [ ] PRD/design/browser plan route names match the implementation
- [ ] `lex link suggest --module <CODE> --json` has no unresolved canonical references before declaring the module done

sections.title: Approved Deviations from Capstone
content: These deviations are intentional and reflect the implemented Actions architecture.
| Aspect | Earlier/Capstone expectation | Actions implementation | Why |
| Tenant isolation | Application-level filtering | PostgreSQL RLS on `TenantId` | Database-enforced isolation |
| Endpoint base | Generic endpoint patterns | `TenantEndpoint` variants with tenant/user/language context | Consistent session context boundary |
| DB schema | Default schema | `actions` schema | Multi-service isolation in shared database |
| Grid list route | POST `/grid` or historical `/list` | Canonical POST `/grid-list`, optional `/grid` and `/server-grid` aliases | Aligns docs, frontend constants, and browser plans |
| Contracts path | Per-entity contract folders | Module-level files in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts` while readable | Lower ceremony for the current module size |
| Feature persistence | All entities under `Data/Entities` | Feature-owned entities/configs under the feature `Shared/` folder | Keeps aggregate behavior and persistence together |
| Commands/mappers | Always extracted command/mapper classes | Endpoint-specific orchestration may stay in the endpoint folder/file | Extract only for reuse, complexity, or testability |
| Feature folder nesting | `Features/{Entity}/` | `Features/Admin/{Entity}/` for admin modules | Separates admin configuration from operational actions |
| Delete request naming | Singular-only naming | Batch delete requests may use plural names | Reflects batch semantics |
| Versioning/soft delete | Not universal | Added per domain need | Avoids unused infrastructure while preserving audit/concurrency where needed |

- [Vertical Slice](api-vertical-slice.md) - Folder structure and file placement
- [Endpoints](api-endpoints.md) - TenantEndpoint base classes, HTTP status mapping
- [API Overview](api-overview.md) - Architecture overview and project structure
